top_pain_points.signal: App crashes after extended use or when submitting tests
Poor technical reliability with recurring need to reinstall
Lack of advanced features like category selection or detailed answer explanations
Inability to track progress due to app logging errors
High cost compared to similar apps with fewer features
Limited functionality in free version (e.g., daily question limits)
Dependency on poor existing tools (e.g., broken Jira management)
Budget constraints for purchasing preferred software
Being forced to revert to basic tools like Excel due to lack of features or convenience
Frustration with errors and lack of official errata/support.
Difficulty with unexpected complexity and steep learning curves in formal software.
Concern about building or buying tools that lack team adoption/traction.

top_desired_outcomes.signal: Passing certification exams (e.g., PMP) with structured, efficient preparation
Reliable, crash-free app performance for consistent study
Clear, detailed explanations for answers to improve understanding
Time-efficient practice for busy professionals balancing work
Progress tracking and readiness assessment for exam dates
Effective tools for project planning and team coordination
Convenient integration with existing workflows (e.g., with OneNote)
Cost-effective solutions compared to alternatives
Accurate, error-free educational or reference materials.
Tools that align with specific, stated needs, not just feature richness.
Software/tools with low friction and high team adoption.
Effective study aids with ample, high-quality practice and clear explanations.

top_deal_breakers.signal: App crashes or technical failures that block usage
Lack of basic features necessary for effective study (e.g., category selection)
Prohibitively high cost without clear added value
Inability to track progress or assess readiness
Poor customer support or developer responsiveness

Uncorrected critical errors in content
Prohibitively high learning curve for unnecessary features
Guaranteed lack of team adoption
Excessive cost without clear value
